sessions.intent: Plan the rename of PLang's `file` module to `path` to match the protocol-agnostic abstraction the type layer already provides.

before.assumptions: Module-name discovery is namespace-driven (source generator reads `namespace app.modules.<name>`), so the rename rides through the catalog automatically. The `http` module stays separate. The `app.types.path.file` namespace (FilePath type) is unrelated to the module rename and stays.
before.risk: Stale `plang` binary causes phantom test failures (`Action 'path.X' not found`) unless a clean rebuild is performed before `plang --test`. Doc sweep must avoid blindly s/file/path/ because `file` appears as an English noun in many places where the reading stays correct.

actions.context: Spine plan: Why section, note-to-coder ownership callout, in-scope list, deferred (NOT in scope) list per conversation, single-stage index, cross-cutting decisions (no shim, namespace-driven discovery, build order, stale-binary trap).

Single mechanical stage. Goal/Scope/Deliverables/Dependencies header, note-to-coder ownership callout, ordered implementation steps (C# first → build → markdown → .goal sweep → docs → clean rebuild → plang build → tests), file-by-file inventory, common-failure guide, verification checklist with concrete grep commands.
Initial summary entry; newest-at-top per architect convention. Captures the Why, the deferred questions settled with Ingi, and the recon impact counts.

Defer HTTP-specific configuration (headers, auth), http.upload/download collapse into path.copy, response metadata on Data.Properties, and POST-vs-PUT default for path.save to HTTP. No real-world pain yet; designing in advance is guessing. This branch is rename-only.

after.status: Plan complete and ready for coder handoff.
after.health: Branch created clean off origin/runtime2. No code edits yet; only architect output under `.bot/rename-file-to-path/architect/`.
after.notes: Next bot: coder. Run: `run.ps1 coder rename-file-to-path "Execute the file→path module rename per architect plan" -b rename-file-to-path`. Stage-1 carries the full mechanical inventory; coder owns the final shape but the Deliverables list is the contract.
